text: Higher average weekly running distance is statistically associated with faster marathon finish times in a dose-response relationship.
Greater number of weekly runs is statistically associated with faster marathon finish times.
Higher maximum weekly running distance achieved during the training block is associated with faster marathon performance.
Completing more runs of 32 km or longer during the pre-marathon training block is associated with faster marathon finish times.
Faster average running pace during training is associated with faster marathon finish times.
Longer longest run distance completed before the marathon is associated with faster finish times.
The rate of non-finishers in the marathon is approximately 7.27%.
